mnemonics.title: T-REX Matrix
mnemonics.content: Translation matrix structure: Top Row 1s, Rightmost Elements X/Y translation. Remember: T-Rex (T for Translation) has 1s on main diagonal (identity), and translations in last column

mnemonics.title: Scale Diagonal
mnemonics.content: Scaling matrix has scale factors ONLY on main diagonal: S_x, S_y, 1 (homogeneous). Visualize diagonal as ruler markings changing size

mnemonics.title: SRT Order
mnemonics.content: Common transformation sequence: Scale -> Rotate -> Translate. Remember 'Scale Before You Move' (Scaling first preserves local coordinates)

mnemonics.title: T-Preserve
mnemonics.content: Translation preserves: Shape, Size, Parallelism. Remember 'T-Preserve' - T for Translation preserves these properties

acronyms.usage: Remember scaling order affects final result when combined with other transformations
Recalls that translation performs vector addition to coordinates
Remember homogeneous coordinates allow combining transformations through matrix multiplication

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a 3x3 grid where the first two diagonal cells are 1s, and the last column contains your translation values. Like ID cards for positions
Picture stretching/shrinking axes - X-axis as horizontal rubber band, Y-axis as vertical spring
Visualize a magic clipboard holding both position (x,y) and transformation instructions (w=1) together
See matrix multiplication as onion layers - each transformation wraps around previous ones

analogies.analogy: Like moving a chess piece on a board - position changes but piece orientation/size remains same
Similar to using a photocopier's zoom function - enlarges/reduces but keeps proportions (if uniform)
Like a Swiss Army knife - single tool (coordinate system) that combines multiple functions (transformations)
